rulings.Case Title: ELEONOR SABALVARO et al VS. REYNALDO P RODRIGUEZ
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANTS ELEONOR SABALVARO and LORNA WONG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED. Demurrer to the first cause of action for promissory estoppel is sustained with leave to amend to allege a clear and unambiguous promise. Demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege an enforceable contract. See Statute of Frauds, Estate of Housley 56 Cal.App.4th 342. Demurrer to the Third cause of action for interference with prospective economic advantage is sustained with leave to amend to allege a type of business relationship that gives rise to this cause of action. See 5 Witkin Summary of CA Law Ch. IX, §741. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ELEONOR SABALVARO et al VS. REYNALDO P RODRIGUEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Cross-Complaint Of Reynaldo P. Rodriguez For Damages
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANTS LORNA RODRIGUEZ WONG and ELEONOR SABALVARO A.K.A. ELEONOR S. LIBBON's Motion To Strike Portions Of First Amended Cross-Complaint Of Reynaldo P. Rodriguez For Damages Moot in part and granted in part. Motion to strike the request for punitive damages in moot in light of ruling on demurrer. Motion to strike request for attorney's fees is granted, non-opposition filed. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUES LANDY VS. ROBERT PIGGOTT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT ROBERT PIGGOTT's Amended Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication DENIED. Moving party failed to shift burden and show that (1) the operative notice was not proper and/or (2) that it has been waived. No authority is cited for the proposition that the operative notice is not proper. Evidence presented re: waiver, does not support the proposition that landlord intended to waive the Notice. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JACQUES LANDY VS. ROBERT PIGGOTT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Further Discovery Responses By Defendant Robert Piggot; Request For Admissions And Genuineness Of Documents, Set One; Form Interrogatories-General, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Motion For An Order To Compel Compliance And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 25, 2014, line 9. PLAINTIFF JACQUES LANDY's Motion For Further Discovery Responses By Defendant Robert Piggot; Request For Admissions And Genuineness Of Documents, Set One; Form Interrogatories-General, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set Two; Motion For An Order To Compel Compliance And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions GRANTED. Objections are overruled. Defendant shall provide responses to Discovery at issue as listed in the separate statement by personal delivery 4:00 p.m. on November 25, 2014. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,200. = (501/REQ)
